perturbation.original_text: Confidential information may only be passed on to general managers, employees, representatives or consultants of the Partner for whom the disclosure of or access to such confidential information is demonstrably necessary in the scope of the project. Insofar as these parties are not already bound to secrecy because of a work contract and/or based on a statutory
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential information may only be passed on to general managers, employees, representatives or consultants of the Partner for whom the disclosure of or access to such confidential information is deemed appropriate by the Partner. Insofar as these parties are not already bound to secrecy because of a work contract and/or based on a statutory
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts disclosure to those with a 'demonstrably necessary' need, aligning with typical NDA standards. The modified text changes this to 'deemed appropriate by the Partner,' giving the Partner broad, unrestricted discretion over who receives confidential information. This weakens the confidentiality protection.
perturbation.law_explanation: Austrian law on business secrets requires reasonable steps to maintain secrecy. Granting unfettered discretion to disclose information weakens these steps, potentially violating the law if the Partner's practices do not align with what is 'reasonable'. Specifically, GeschGehG § 4 relates to actions against the violation of business secrets, premising this on a valid business secret deserving protection. This is weakened with broad discretion to disclose.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 4 GeschGehG - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) § 4 Handlungsverbote (1) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht erlangt werden durch 1. unbefugten Zugang zu, unbefugte Aneignung oder unbefugtes Kopieren von Dokumenten, Gegenständen, Materialien, Stoffen oder elektronischen Dateien, die der rechtmäßigen Kontrolle des Inhabers des Geschäftsgeheimnisses unterliegen und die das Geschäftsgeheimnis enthalten oder aus denen sich das Geschäftsgeheimnis ableiten lässt, oder 2. jedes sonstige Verhalten, das unter den jeweiligen Umständen nicht dem Grundsatz von Treu und Glauben unter Berücksichtigung der anständigen Marktgepflogenheit entspricht. (2) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht nutzen oder offenlegen, wer 1. das Geschäftsgeheimnis durch eine eigene Handlung nach Absatz 1 a) Nummer 1 oder b) Nummer 2 erlangt hat, 2. gegen eine Verpflichtung zur Beschränkung der Nutzung des Geschäftsgeheimnisses verstößt oder 3. gegen eine Verpflichtung verstößt, das Geschäftsgeheimnis nicht offenzulegen. (3) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf nicht erlangen, nutzen oder offenlegen, wer das Geschäftsgeheimnis über eine andere Person erlangt hat und zum Zeitpunkt der Erlangung, Nutzung oder Offenlegung weiß oder wissen müsste, dass diese das Geschäftsgeheimnis entgegen Absatz 2 genutzt oder offengelegt hat. Das gilt insbesondere, wenn die Nutzung in der Herstellung, dem Anbieten, dem Inverkehrbringen oder der Einfuhr, der Ausfuhr oder der Lagerung für diese Zwecke von rechtsverletzenden Produkten besteht. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified text, by replacing 'demonstrably necessary' with 'deemed appropriate by the Partner,' introduces a contradiction to the principle of maintaining business secrecy under Austrian law. The original text aligned more closely with taking 'reasonable steps to maintain secrecy' as described in the Law Explanation. The modified text grants the Partner unfettered discretion, weakening the protection of the business secret. Scraped Law Snippet 1, § 4 GeschGehG, outlines prohibited actions related to business secrets, including 'unbefugten Zugang' (unauthorized access) and behavior that does not align with 'Treu und Glauben' (good faith) considering 'anständigen Marktgepflogenheit' (decent market practice). Giving the Partner broad discretion to disclose information significantly increases the risk of unauthorized access or disclosure that doesn't align with good faith or decent market practice. The change creates a legal contradiction, since the altered term conflicts with legally protecting business secrets.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise provided for in this agreement, the above- mentioned obligations of this Section II shall apply in each case for a period of 3 (three) years after the point in time at which the respective confidential information was disclosed by M&R or made accessible to the Partner.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise provided for in this agreement, the above- mentioned obligations of this Section II shall apply in each case for a period of 1 (one) year after the point in time at which the respective confidential information was disclosed by M&R or made accessible to the Partner.
perturbation.explanation: Reducing the confidentiality period from 3 years to 1 year weakens the protection of confidential information. This may not align with the reasonable expectations for protecting certain types of business secrets, especially those with long-term value.
perturbation.law_explanation: Austrian Law on Business Secrets requires adequate measures to protect confidential information. A one-year confidentiality period might be deemed insufficient for certain trade secrets, especially in industries with long product development cycles, thus not meeting the 'adequate measures' requirement implicitly required by the law defining business secrets (GeschGehG § 2 Z 1).
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 2 GeschGehG - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) § 2 Begriffsbestimmungen Im Sinne dieses Gesetzes ist 1. Geschäftsgeheimnis eine Information a) die weder insgesamt noch in der genauen Anordnung und Zusammensetzung ihrer Bestandteile den Personen in den Kreisen, die üblicherweise mit dieser Art von Informationen umgehen, allgemein bekannt oder ohne Weiteres zugänglich ist und daher von wirtschaftlichem Wert ist und b) die Gegenstand von den Umständen nach angemessenen Geheimhaltungsmaßnahmen durch ihren rechtmäßigen Inhaber ist und c) bei der ein berechtigtes Interesse an der Geheimhaltung besteht; 2. Inhaber eines Geschäftsgeheimnisses jede natürliche oder juristische Person, die die rechtmäßige Kontrolle über ein Geschäftsgeheimnis hat; 3. Rechtsverletzer jede natürliche oder juristische Person, die entgegen § 4 ein Geschäftsgeheimnis rechtswidrig erlangt, nutzt oder offenlegt; Rechtsverletzer ist nicht, wer sich auf eine Ausnahme nach § 5 berufen kann; 4. rechtsverletzendes Produkt ein Produkt, dessen Konzeption, Merkmale, Funktionsweise, Herstellungsprozess oder Marketing in erheblichem Umfang auf einem rechtswidrig erlangten, genutzten oder offengelegten Geschäftsgeheimnis beruht. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The changed text reduces the confidentiality period from 3 years to 1 year, which contradicts the requirement for 'angemessenen Geheimhaltungsmaßnahmen' (reasonable confidentiality measures) as stated in § 2 Z 1 b of the GeschGehG (Law Snippet 1). The Perturbation Explanation states that a one-year period may be insufficient, especially for trade secrets with long-term value. The Law Explanation supports this by stating that a one-year period may not meet the 'adequate measures' requirement. Thus, the modification creates a legal contradiction by potentially failing to provide adequate protection for business secrets as required by Austrian law.

perturbation.original_text: Excepted from the non- disclosure obligation above shall be know -how and information a) which was apparent to or generally known by the Partner or was state of the art already; b) which was already known by the Partner at the time of disclosure; c) which subsequent ly becomes apparent or generally known or state of the art through no fault of the Partner; d) which is disclosed or made accessible to the Partner by a third party who has the right to do so; e) regarding which M&R previously agreed to a passing on, disclosure or making accessible to third parties in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: Excepted from the non- disclosure obligation above shall be know -how and information a) which was apparent to or generally known by the Partner or was state of the art already; b) which was already known by the Partner at the time of disclosure; c) which subsequently becomes apparent or generally known or state of the art; d) which is disclosed or made accessible to the Partner by a third party; e) regarding which M&R was aware of its disclosure and did not object in writing.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text removes the 'through no fault of the Partner' clause in (c) and the 'who has the right to do so' clause in (d) and changes (e) from 'previously agreed' to 'was aware of'. This means the Partner is not responsible if they cause the information to become public knowledge and can disclose information if a third party discloses it, regardless of their right to do so. It also weakens M&R's control, only requiring awareness instead of agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: According to Austrian Law on Business Secrets, the holder of a business secret has the right to prevent the unlawful acquisition, use, or disclosure of the business secret (GeschGehG § 3). By removing the fault clause and requiring only awareness for disclosure, the contract weakens the protection afforded by the law, as M&R might not be able to prevent disclosure even if the Partner acted negligently or a third party unlawfully disclosed the information. If the Partner contributed to the secret becoming public, or a third party had no right to disclose, then it could still violate the spirit of protection in the law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 3 GeschGehG - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) § 3 Erlaubte Handlungen (1) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf insbesondere erlangt werden durch 1. eine eigenständige Entdeckung oder Schöpfung; 2. ein Beobachten, Untersuchen, Rückbauen oder Testen eines Produkts oder Gegenstands, das oder der a) öffentlich verfügbar gemacht wurde oder b) sich im rechtmäßigen Besitz des Beobachtenden, Untersuchenden, Rückbauenden oder Testenden befindet und dieser keiner Pflicht zur Beschränkung der Erlangung des Geschäftsgeheimnisses unterliegt; 3. ein Ausüben von Informations- und Anhörungsrechten der Arbeitnehmer oder Mitwirkungs- und Mitbestimmungsrechte der Arbeitnehmervertretung. (2) Ein Geschäftsgeheimnis darf erlangt, genutzt oder offengelegt werden, wenn dies durch Gesetz, aufgrund eines Gesetzes oder durch Rechtsgeschäft gestattet ist. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the principles outlined in the provided information regarding the protection of business secrets under Austrian law. The original text provided exceptions to the non-disclosure agreement only when the partner was not at fault for the information becoming public, a third party had the right to disclose it, or M&R had previously agreed to the disclosure. The modified text weakens these protections by removing the 'no fault' clause, allowing disclosure regardless of the third party's right, and changing 'previously agreed' to 'was aware of', thus reducing M&R's control over their business secrets. The Law Explanation supports the view that these changes weaken the legal protections afforded to business secrets.

perturbation.original_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for the damage caused and shall keep M&R indemnified from all resulting claims and costs. If it is certain that confidential information found its way to third parties from the sphere of the Partner, a culpable (at least negligent) breach of non- disclosure obligations shall be assumed - except when there is evidence to t he contrary.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of a culpable breach, the Partner shall be liable to M&R for compensation for direct damages only. If confidential information is disclosed, the Partner's liability is capped at €10,000, regardless of actual damages.
perturbation.explanation: The original text allows for compensation for all damages. The modified text limits compensation to direct damages and caps liability at a fixed amount. This weakens M&R's ability to recover for all losses resulting from a breach of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Austrian General Civil Code (Allgemeines bürgerliches Gesetzbuch - ABGB)
perturbation.law_explanation: ABGB § 1324 states that anyone who causes damages to another through their fault is liable to compensate them. While parties can agree to limit liability, such limitations must be reasonable and not grossly disproportionate to the potential damages. Capping liability at a low amount like €10,000 could be deemed unreasonable if the actual damages from a confidentiality breach are significantly higher, potentially making this provision unenforceable under Austrian law.

perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the Partner's liability to 'direct damages only' and caps it at €10,000, regardless of actual damages. This contradicts ABGB § 1324, as explained by the LLM, which states that anyone who causes damages to another through their fault is liable to compensate them. While the LLM notes that liability limitations are possible, it argues that a €10,000 cap could be unreasonable if the actual damages are significantly higher. Scraped Law Snippet 2 includes '§ 1324 ABGB Insbesondere (§§ 1325 – 1341) 1) bey Verletzungen an dem Körper (§§ 1325 – 1327)' among other things indicating ABGB § 1324 deals with liability for damages. The modification weakens the ability of M&R to recover for losses resulting from a breach of confidentiality. Therefore, the modified text introduces a significant limitation on liability that could be deemed unenforceable under Austrian law, creating a legal contradiction based on misaligned terminology.

perturbation.original_text: This agreement shall become effective upon signing and has a term of 5 (five) years. Without prejudice to the provision in Section II, last paragraph, the non -disclosure obligation for business secrets and know -how shall, however, continue to appl y after termination of this agreement unless one of the exceptions described in Section III , Subsection c), d) or e) has occurred, for which the burden of proof lies with the Partner.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement shall become effective upon signing and has a term of 1 (one) year. Upon termination, all obligations under this agreement cease, including the non-disclosure obligations.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement provides for a 5-year term with ongoing confidentiality obligations for business secrets and know-how even after termination. The modified text reduces the term to 1 year and eliminates any post-termination confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.law_explanation: Austrian law on business secrets (GeschGehG § 5) aims to protect business secrets even after the termination of a relationship, preventing unfair competition. By terminating all confidentiality obligations after one year, the modified text weakens the protection of M&R's business secrets, potentially allowing the Partner to freely use or disclose those secrets after the agreement expires, even if that harms M&R's competitive position. The law expects reasonable measures to protect trade secrets, which is undermined by this short term. If the trade secret has a longer lifespan or value, a one year protection would be inadequate.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: § 5 GeschGehG - Einzelnorm zurück weiter Nichtamtliches Inhaltsverzeichnis Gesetz zum Schutz von Geschäftsgeheimnissen (GeschGehG) § 5 Ausnahmen Die Erlangung, die Nutzung oder die Offenlegung eines Geschäftsgeheimnisses fällt nicht unter die Verbote des § 4, wenn dies zum Schutz eines berechtigten Interesses erfolgt, insbesondere 1. zur Ausübung des Rechts der freien Meinungsäußerung und der Informationsfreiheit, einschließlich der Achtung der Freiheit und der Pluralität der Medien; 2. zur Aufdeckung einer rechtswidrigen Handlung oder eines beruflichen oder sonstigen Fehlverhaltens, wenn die Erlangung, Nutzung oder Offenlegung geeignet ist, das allgemeine öffentliche Interesse zu schützen; 3. im Rahmen der Offenlegung durch Arbeitnehmer gegenüber der Arbeitnehmervertretung, wenn dies erforderlich ist, damit die Arbeitnehmervertretung ihre Aufgaben erfüllen kann. zum Seitenanfang Impressum Datenschutz Barrierefreiheitserklärung Feedback-Formular

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts Austrian law on business secrets by eliminating post-termination confidentiality obligations. The original agreement maintained these obligations, aligning with the intent to protect business secrets even after the agreement's end. The modified text's one-year term and cessation of all obligations upon termination weakens the protection of business secrets and know-how, potentially allowing the Partner to freely use or disclose them, harming M&R's competitive position. The scraped snippets reference the GeschGehG, the law concerning business secrets. The Law Explanation indicates this change undermines the expected reasonable measures to protect trade secrets.
